 {"id":"bd-3oaig.2","title":"Interaction model + keymap spec for mega screen","description":"Goal:
- Define the full interaction model: keybindings, modes, and on-screen help.

- Keymap table covering: sample selection, complexity knobs, layout mode, sizing, zoom/pan, debug overlays, auto-fit, and perf sweep.
- Mode semantics (e.g., edit/inspect/auto-run) and conflict resolution rules.

Considerations:
- Keep bindings consistent with existing showcase conventions.
- Provide both single-step changes and auto-animate modes.

Acceptance:
- Keymap documented in-code and drives help overlay rendering.
- Every interactive knob has a visible state indicator.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:31:28.992591041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T16:46:44.579247403Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T16:46:44.579229830Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mermaid","showcase","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:31:28.992591041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:34:12.664811873Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":749,"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented interaction model + keymap spec in mermaid.rs.  {"id":"bd-3oaig.3","title":"Mega screen shell + layout skeleton","description":"Goal:
- Build the base screen composition: viewport, side panels, footer/status, and help overlay regions.

Deliverables:
- Screen struct + state skeleton in ftui-demo-showcase.
- Panel layout that works at 80x24 and 120x40 (minimum sizes).

Considerations:
- Reserve space for metrics + sample metadata without obscuring the diagram.
- Panels should degrade gracefully when height/width are tight.

Acceptance:
- Screen renders with placeholder data and respects existing demo navigation.
- Layout sizes are deterministic and tested with snapshots.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:31:35.484792335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T16:47:21.410815205Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mermaid","showcase","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:31:35.484792335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3oaig.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3oaig.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:34:20.249752009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-ukp1f.2","title":"Canvas: node shape rendering (all 8 shapes via Painter)","description":"## What
Implement Canvas-based rendering for all 8 NodeShape variants using Painter primitives from canvas.rs.

## Shape Mapping
- Rect → Painter::rect (filled rectangle with border)
- Rounded → Painter::rect with corner radius (approximate with arcs or short diagonals)
- Stadium → Painter::rect with semicircle ends (half-circles at left/right)
- Subroutine → Double-bordered rect (two nested rectangles)
- Diamond → Painter polygon: 4 vertices at (cx, top), (right, cy), (cx, bottom), (left, cy)
- Hexagon → Painter polygon: 6 vertices (flat-top or pointy-top orientation)
- Circle → Painter::circle centered on node, radius = min(width, height) / 2
- Asymmetric → Painter polygon: flag shape (rect with pointed right side)

## Implementation Details
- Each shape gets a dedicated fn: canvas_draw_rect, canvas_draw_diamond, canvas_draw_circle, etc.
- Fill interior with node's theme background color via Painter::fill
- Draw border in node's theme border color via Painter::line or Painter::polygon
- Scale from layout units to canvas pixel coordinates via Viewport transform
- Padding between border and label text must account for shape geometry (diamond has less usable interior than rect)

## Files to Modify
- crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_render.rs (add canvas_render_node method)
- crates/ftui-extras/src/canvas.rs (if new primitives needed, e.g., polygon fill)

## Tests
- Unit test per shape: verify correct canvas pixels set for known input coordinates
- Snapshot tests: all 8 shapes rendered at 80×24 in Braille mode
- Property test: shape bounding box never exceeds allocated node rectangle","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:26:52.700733389Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:16.823650122Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["canvas","mermaid","rendering"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1emsq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:15.758468627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1qkni","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:16.823571945Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ukp1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:26:52.700733389Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ukp1f.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:13.660002036Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ukp1f.3","title":"Canvas: text label compositing over canvas layer","description":"## What
Composite text labels (node labels, edge labels, cluster titles) on top of canvas-rendered shapes. Canvas draws geometry in sub-cell dots; text must be rendered in the cell layer on top.

## Implementation
- Canvas rendering writes to a CanvasLayer (Braille/Block/HalfBlock dots).
- Text rendering writes to the Buffer cell layer (standard Cell writes).
- When compositing: cells with text take priority over canvas dots in the same cell. This is how canvas.rs already works — Painter writes to a separate pixel grid that gets merged into Buffer cells, and explicit cell writes override.
- Center node labels within the shape's usable interior area (accounting for shape geometry — diamond has triangular corners, circle has curved edges).
- Edge labels: position at midpoint of edge, write text centered.
- Wrap long labels using existing wrap_text function.

## Key Design Decision
- Do NOT try to render text as Braille dots. Cell-based text is always more readable.
- Background color under text should match the node fill for readability.

## Files
- crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_render.rs (add canvas_composite_labels)

## Tests
- Unit: label centered within various shapes at different sizes
- Snapshot: nodes with single-line and multi-line labels in Canvas mode","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:26:52.960464261Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:14.445853490Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["canvas","mermaid","rendering"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ukp1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:26:52.960464261Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ukp1f.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:14.445816651Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ukp1f.4","title":"Canvas: snapshot tests + performance benchmarks","description":"## What
Comprehensive test suite for Canvas-mode diagram rendering.

## Snapshot Tests
- All 8 node shapes in Braille mode at 80×24
- All 8 node shapes in Block mode at 80×24
- 5-node flowchart in Braille at 80×24 and 120×40
- 10-node dense graph with crossing edges in Braille at 120×40
- Mixed shapes diagram (diamond decision + rect process + circle event) at 120×40
- Edge styles (solid, dashed, dotted, thick) in Braille mode
- Arrowhead types (normal, open, circle, diamond) in Braille mode

## Performance Tests
- Benchmark: 50-node graph render time in Braille vs Cell mode
- Assert: Canvas mode < 2× Cell mode render time
- Profile: identify any hot spots in Canvas ↔ Buffer compositing

## Determinism Tests
- Property test: same input + same mode = identical output (byte-for-byte)
- Cross-mode test: Canvas and Cell modes produce diagrams with same node positions (only visual fidelity differs)

## Files
- crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_render.rs (tests module)
- crates/ftui-extras/tests/snapshots/ (new .snap files)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:26:53.286762256Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:15.494821331Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["canvas","mermaid","rendering","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ukp1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:26:53.286762256Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ukp1f.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:14.713851018Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ukp1f.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:15.494780394Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ukp1f.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:15.238569856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ukp1f.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:14.976904184Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-ukp1f.5","title":"Canvas: triangular arrowhead rendering","description":"## What
Render arrowheads as filled triangles at edge endpoints using Painter polygon fill, instead of '>' / '<' / 'v' / '^' characters.

## Implementation
- Compute arrowhead direction from the last two waypoints of each edge.
- Triangle vertices: tip at endpoint, two base vertices perpendicular to edge direction, spread by configurable width (default 3-4 canvas pixels).
- Fill the triangle with the edge color.
- Support arrow types: normal (filled triangle), open (outline only), circle (small filled circle), cross (X mark), diamond (small filled diamond).

## Edge Cases
- Very short edges: skip arrowhead if edge length < arrowhead size.
- Bidirectional edges: arrowheads at both ends.
- Self-loops: arrowhead at the loop's return point.

## Files
- crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_render.rs (add canvas_render_arrowhead)
- crates/ftui-extras/src/canvas.rs (add polygon_fill if not present)

## Tests
- Unit: arrowhead pointing in all 8 cardinal/ordinal directions
- Visual snapshot: edges with different arrow types","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:26:53.452291041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T16:41:39.970753766Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["canvas","mermaid","rendering"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.5","depends_on_id":"bd-ukp1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:26:53.452291041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.5","depends_on_id":"bd-ukp1f.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:14.184012018Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-uo6v","title":"Create virtualization performance tests","description":"# Create virtualization performance tests

## Background
Part of Progressive Rendering (bd-3t87). Performance tests ensure virtualization achieves O(visible) not O(total) complexity.

## Performance Benchmarks

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod benchmarks {
    use criterion::{black_box, criterion_group, criterion_main, Criterion};
    
    fn bench_render_100k_items(c: &mut Criterion) {
        let mut group = c.benchmark_group("virtualized_list");
        
        // Generate 100K log lines
        let items: Vec<LogLine> = (0..100_000)
            .map(|i| LogLine::new(format!("Log line {}", i)))
            .collect();
        
        let virtualized = Virtualized::new(items);
        let list = VirtualizedList::new(virtualized);
        let mut buf = Buffer::empty(Rect::new(0, 0, 80, 24));
        let budget = RenderBudget::unlimited();
        
        group.bench_function("render_visible_only", |b| {
            b.iter(|| {
                list.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 80, 24), &mut buf, &budget);
                black_box(&buf);
            });
        });
        
        group.finish();
    }
    
    fn bench_scroll_100k_items(c: &mut Criterion) {
        let mut group = c.benchmark_group("scroll");
        
        let items: Vec<LogLine> = (0..100_000)
            .map(|i| LogLine::new(format!("Log line {}", i)))
            .collect();
        
        let mut virtualized = Virtualized::new(items);
        
        group.bench_function("scroll_one_line", |b| {
            b.iter(|| {
                virtualized.scroll(1);
                black_box(virtualized.scroll_offset);
            });
        });
        
        group.bench_function("scroll_page", |b| {
            b.iter(|| {
                virtualized.page_down();
                black_box(virtualized.scroll_offset);
            });
        });
        
        group.finish();
    }
    
    fn bench_append_with_ring(c: &mut Criterion) {
        let mut group = c.benchmark_group("log_ring");
        
        let mut ring = LogRing::new(10_000);
        
        group.bench_function("append_single", |b| {
            b.iter(|| {
                ring.push(LogLine::new("New log line"));
            });
        });
        
        group.bench_function("append_batch_100", |b| {
            b.iter(|| {
                for i in 0..100 {
                    ring.push(LogLine::new(format!("Batch line {}", i)));
                }
            });
        });
        
        group.finish();
    }
    
    criterion_group!(
        benches,
        bench_render_100k_items,
        bench_scroll_100k_items,
        bench_append_with_ring,
    );
    criterion_main!(benches);
}
```

## Performance Requirements

| Operation | Target | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| Render 100K items | < 20ms | Only visible items rendered |
| Scroll 1 line | < 1μs | O(1) offset update |
| Page scroll | < 1μs | O(1) offset update |
| Append single | < 100ns | Amortized O(1) |
| Memory (100K items) | < 50MB | With 10K ring capacity |

## Unit Tests

```rust
#[test]
fn render_time_scales_with_visible_not_total() {
    // 1K items
    let small = create_list(1_000);
    let start = Instant::now();
    small.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 80, 24), &mut buf, &budget);
    let small_time = start.elapsed();
    
    // 100K items
    let large = create_list(100_000);
    let start = Instant::now();
    large.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 80, 24), &mut buf, &budget);
    let large_time = start.elapsed();
    
    // Large should be similar to small (both only render ~24 items)
    assert!(large_time < small_time * 2);
}

#[test]
fn memory_bounded_by_ring_capacity() {
    let mut ring = LogRing::new(1_000);
    
    // Add 100K items
    for i in 0..100_000 {
        ring.push(LogLine::new(format!("Line {}", i)));
    }
    
    // Only 1K in memory
    assert_eq!(ring.len(), 1_000);
    assert_eq!(ring.total_count(), 100_000);
}

#[test]
fn scrollbar_position_accurate() {
    let mut v = Virtualized::new((0..100).collect::<Vec<_>>());
    v.visible_count = 10;
    
    // At start
    v.scroll_offset = 0;
    assert_eq!(v.scrollbar_position(), 0.0);
    
    // At end
    v.scroll_offset = 90;
    assert_eq!(v.scrollbar_position(), 1.0);
    
    // At middle
    v.scroll_offset = 45;
    assert!((v.scrollbar_position() - 0.5).abs() < 0.1);
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All benchmarks meet targets
- [ ] Render time O(visible)
- [ ] Memory bounded by capacity
- [ ] Scroll operations O(1)
- [ ] No allocations during scroll","notes":"## Property Tests

Add property tests for virtualization invariants:

```rust
use proptest::prelude::*;

proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn scroll_offset_always_valid(
        total_items in 1usize..100_000,
        visible_count in 1usize..100,
        scroll_delta in -1000i32..1000
    ) {
        let mut v = Virtualized::new((0..total_items).collect::<Vec<_>>());
        v.visible_count = visible_count;
        
        v.scroll(scroll_delta);
        
        // Scroll offset must be valid
        prop_assert!(v.scroll_offset < total_items);
        prop_assert!(v.scroll_offset + v.visible_count <= total_items + v.visible_count);
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn visible_range_always_within_bounds(
        total_items in 0usize..100_000,
        visible_count in 1usize..100,
        scroll_offset in 0usize..100_000
    ) {
        let mut v = Virtualized::new((0..total_items).collect::<Vec<_>>());
        v.visible_count = visible_count;
        v.scroll_offset = scroll_offset.min(total_items.saturating_sub(1));
        
        let (start, end) = v.visible_range(visible_count as u16);
        
        prop_assert!(start <= end);
        prop_assert!(end <= total_items);
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn ring_never_exceeds_capacity(
        capacity in 1usize..10_000,
        insertions in 0usize..100_000
    ) {
        let mut ring = LogRing::new(capacity);
        
        for i in 0..insertions {
            ring.push(LogLine::new(format!("Line {}", i)));
        }
        
        prop_assert!(ring.len() <= capacity);
    }
}
```

These property tests ensure virtualization invariants hold under all conditions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:20:43.392973094Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T00:57:34.531721995Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T00:57:34.531640772Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-uo6v","depends_on_id":"bd-20wz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-v6y","title":"Implement ASCII width fast-path optimization","description":"# Implement ASCII Width Fast-Path Optimization

## Background
The plan emphasizes that ASCII must dominate common workloads:
- Section 0.12: "ASCII fast-path must remain allocation-free and dominate common workloads"
- Section 4.3: ASCII width is a major optimization opportunity

Most harness/log output is ASCII. For ASCII:
- display width == byte length
- no grapheme segmentation needed
- no Unicode tables needed

## Scope
- Provide a fast, allocation-free `ascii_width` / `display_width` helper.
- Integrate it into text measurement / wrapping / truncation hot paths.

## Design
### Core API
```rust
/// Returns Some(width) if s is pure ASCII; otherwise None.
#[inline]
pub fn ascii_width(s: &str) -> Option<usize> {
    let bytes = s.as_bytes();
    if bytes.iter().all(|&b| b < 0x80) {
        Some(bytes.len())
    } else {
        None
    }
}

#[inline]
pub fn display_width(s: &str) -> usize {
    ascii_width(s).unwrap_or_else(|| unicode_width::UnicodeWidthStr::width(s))
}
```

### Performance notes
- ASCII detection is O(n) but extremely cheap (tight loop over bytes).
- Optional micro-optimizations (still safe): use `memchr` or portable SIMD via `std::simd` if available.
- **Repo policy: unsafe is forbidden**, so any SIMD path must be safe or it is out of scope.

## Integration points
- Segment/Text measurement
- Wrapping/truncation (byte-fast for ASCII)
- Viewport/log viewer rendering

## Tests
- Pure ASCII returns correct width
- Mixed ASCII/Unicode returns None from `ascii_width`
- Empty string returns Some(0)
- Integration tests: wrapping/truncation correctness unchanged

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `ascii_width` and `display_width` exist.
- [ ] ASCII path is allocation-free.
- [ ] Non-ASCII path matches `unicode_width`.
- [ ] Integrated into the ftui-text measurement code paths.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:52:10.389324927Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T18:11:02.790245126Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T18:11:02.790179993Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-v6y","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-v6y","depends_on_id":"bd-1oz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-v6y","depends_on_id":"bd-2uk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-zlq9","title":"Add momentum scrolling with velocity tracking","description":"# Add momentum scrolling with velocity tracking

## Background
Part of Progressive Rendering (bd-3t87). Smooth scrolling requires velocity tracking and momentum continuation.

## Technical Design

```rust
impl<T> Virtualized<T> {
    /// Scroll by delta (positive = down)
    pub fn scroll(&mut self, delta: i32) {
        let new_offset = (self.scroll_offset as i32 + delta)
            .max(0)
            .min(self.len().saturating_sub(1) as i32);
        self.scroll_offset = new_offset as usize;
        
        // Cancel follow mode if scrolling up
        if delta < 0 {
            self.follow_mode = false;
        }
    }
    
    /// Scroll to specific item
    pub fn scroll_to(&mut self, idx: usize) {
        self.scroll_offset = idx.min(self.len().saturating_sub(1));
        self.follow_mode = false;
    }
    
    /// Scroll to bottom (enable follow mode)
    pub fn scroll_to_bottom(&mut self) {
        if self.len() > self.visible_count {
            self.scroll_offset = self.len() - self.visible_count;
        }
    }
    
    /// Start momentum scroll with initial velocity
    pub fn fling(&mut self, velocity: f32) {
        self.scroll_velocity = velocity;
        if velocity > 0.0 {
            self.follow_mode = false;
        }
    }
    
    /// Update momentum (call each frame)
    pub fn tick(&mut self, dt: Duration) {
        if self.scroll_velocity.abs() > 0.5 {
            // Apply velocity
            let delta = (self.scroll_velocity * dt.as_secs_f32() * 60.0) as i32;
            self.scroll(delta);
            
            // Apply friction
            self.scroll_velocity *= 0.92;
        } else {
            self.scroll_velocity = 0.0;
        }
    }
    
    /// Enable/disable follow mode
    pub fn set_follow(&mut self, follow: bool) {
        self.follow_mode = follow;
        if follow {
            self.scroll_to_bottom();
        }
    }
    
    /// Check if at bottom
    pub fn is_at_bottom(&self) -> bool {
        self.scroll_offset + self.visible_count >= self.len()
    }
    
    /// Called when items are added
    pub fn on_items_added(&mut self, count: usize) {
        // Update external len if needed
        if let VirtualizedStorage::External { len, .. } = &mut self.items {
            *len += count;
        }
        
        if self.follow_mode {
            self.scroll_to_bottom();
        }
    }
    
    /// Page up/down
    pub fn page_up(&mut self) {
        self.scroll(-(self.visible_count as i32));
    }
    
    pub fn page_down(&mut self) {
        self.scroll(self.visible_count as i32);
    }
    
    /// Home/End
    pub fn scroll_to_start(&mut self) {
        self.scroll_offset = 0;
        self.follow_mode = false;
    }
    
    pub fn scroll_to_end(&mut self) {
        self.scroll_to_bottom();
        self.follow_mode = true;
    }
}

/// Scroll input handling
impl<T> Virtualized<T> {
    pub fn handle_scroll_event(&mut self, event: &Event) {
        match event {
            Event::Mouse(MouseEvent::ScrollUp { .. }) => self.scroll(-3),
            Event::Mouse(MouseEvent::ScrollDown { .. }) => self.scroll(3),
            Event::Key(KeyCode::Up, _) => self.scroll(-1),
            Event::Key(KeyCode::Down, _) => self.scroll(1),
            Event::Key(KeyCode::PageUp, _) => self.page_up(),
            Event::Key(KeyCode::PageDown, _) => self.page_down(),
            Event::Key(KeyCode::Home, _) => self.scroll_to_start(),
            Event::Key(KeyCode::End, _) => self.scroll_to_end(),
            _ => {}
        }
    }
}
```

## Momentum Physics
- Velocity in items/second
- Friction coefficient: 0.92 per frame
- Threshold: stop when |velocity| < 0.5
- Smooth deceleration over ~20 frames

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scroll by delta works
- [ ] Page up/down work
- [ ] Home/End work
- [ ] Momentum scrolling smooth
- [ ] Follow mode auto-scrolls on add
- [ ] Manual scroll cancels follow","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:20:41.092101550Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T00:46:29.143532773Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T00:46:29.143444948Z","close_reason":"Momentum scrolling with velocity tracking implemented and tested","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zlq9","depends_on_id":"bd-14s3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
